Removing fake media URLs (they're `example.com` placeholders, not presigned S3 objects — they'll fail validation or playback). Improving program titles, descriptions, session titles, and tags. Requirements only mandate counts (2 creators, 3 programs each, ~10 sessions), not placeholder naming.

input.command: cd /Users/subhan/Work/wellspring-subhan-ahmed/backend && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test 2>&1
input.description: Typecheck and run backend Jest tests

content.text:
Here is a concise answer and what was changed.

### Media in seed

Those URLs were never real uploads: they pointed at `example.com` with a `/tenants/{id}/media/...` path, so they are not your S3 bucket, not presigned, and will confuse the app or the browser.

**Removing them is the right default.** The brief still expects you to support **presigned upload** in the product; it does **not** require seeded files. Reviewers can attach media through the normal flow after seeding.

If you ever wanted “something playable in the list” without S3, you would need either a **public** URL that also satisfies your **`mediaUrl` tenant-prefix rules** (usually impractical) or a **dev-only** exception in validation—both are worse than empty `mediaUrl` for a take-home.

`seed.ts` now sets **`mediaUrl` and `mediaType` to `null`** for every seeded session.

### “Creator 1 / Program 1 / Seed” naming

**Not required.** [`docs/REQUIREMENTS.md`](docs/REQUIREMENTS.md) only specifies **2 creators, 3 programs each, ~10 sessions per program**—not titles, tags, or copy. The brief even uses realistic **example program names** (“30-Day Sleep Reset”, “Beginner Yoga Foundations”); matching that style is nicer for demos.

The seed was updated so each tenant gets three named programs (aligned with those examples where it fits), session titles use phase labels plus the program name, tags rotate through wellness-style pairs, and the **`seed` tag** is gone. Logins stay **`creator1@wellspring.example`** / **`creator2@wellspring.example`** with **`Password123!`** so the README stays accurate.

If you want **emails** to look less synthetic too (e.g. `nora@wellspring.example`), say so and we can change seed + README together.

[REDACTED]

input.command: cd /Users/subhan/Work/wellspring-subhan-ahmed/backend && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit && pnpm test 2>&1 | tail -30

content.text:
Here is what changed and why.

The brief only requires **two tenants and the right counts**; nothing says logins must be `creator1@…`. Generic numbered accounts are a common shortcut, but they read as test data. Swapping them for **named personas plus plausible emails** makes demos and screenshots feel like a real product without touching the schema (there is still no separate “display name” on `Creator`—only `email`).

### Changes

1. **`backend/src/prisma/seed.ts`**  
   - Replaced the numeric loop with a **`SEED_CREATORS`** list: **Nora Chen** (`nora.chen@wellspring.example`) and **Marcus Ortiz** (`marcus.ortiz@wellspring.example`), each with the same three program definitions as before.  
   - Seed logs use **person names** and **program titles** instead of “creator 1/2” and “program index”.  
   - Final credential log is now `{ personaName, email }[]` plus the shared password hint.

2. **`README.md`**  
   - Documented the new seeded emails and **`Password123!`**.

After pulling, run **`pnpm db:seed`** again so existing DB rows pick up the new emails and content (or reset the DB first if you rely on a clean slate).

If you later want the **admin UI** to show “Nora Chen” instead of only the address bar / email field, that would need a **`Creator` profile field** and a small migration—say the word if you want that scope.

[REDACTED]

Prisma also printed a deprecation notice about `package.json#prisma`; it is informational and did not stop the seed.

## Summary

Brings `develop` up to date on `main`, including the improved Prisma seed (realistic demo tenants and program catalog, no fake media URLs) and other commits already on `develop`.

## Test plan

- [ ] CI green on this PR
- [ ] Smoke: `pnpm db:seed`, login with seeded accounts, list programs/sessions
EOF
)" 2>&1

input.command: cd /Users/subhan/Work/wellspring-subhan-ahmed && gh pr view 1 --json state,mergedAt,url 2>&1 && git fetch origin main && git log -1 --oneline origin/main
input.description: Verify PR merged and show origin/main tip

content.text:
Here is what ran:

1. **Pushed** `develop` to `origin` (it was several commits ahead of `origin/develop`).
2. **Opened** pull request **#1**: [Merge develop into main](https://github.com/subhandev/wellspring-subhan-ahmed/pull/1) (`develop` → `main`).
3. **Merged** that PR with a **merge commit** (branch `develop` was not deleted).

`origin/main` now points at **`7990152`** (`Merge develop into main`).

To refresh your local `main`:

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull origin main
```

Your local `develop` may still have **uncommitted `README.md` edits**; those were never committed, so they are **not** on `main` from this merge.
